---
title: "Figure 5 — domain boundaries overlaid on IR's residual distortion (validation)"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
Honest test of the domain classification: does IR's RESIDUAL distortion (the emission non-Gaussianity, the
only distortion IR has, since it accounts for the temporal correlation) concentrate where the domain
boundaries say? Colour = affine-invariant distance of the information-distortion matrix from the identity
(0 = perfectly Gaussian; larger = more non-Gaussian), macro_IR, from battery_sim. Overlaid:
  dashed diagonal  N_ch * (Delta/tau) = 1   (telegraph boundary, below-left = few transitions/interval)
  dashed horizontal N_ch = 30               (multinomial boundary, below = few channels)
Faceted by noise to expose the noise dependence the static schematic hides. Reading: the distortion does
grow toward the bottom-left (ordering validated), but it is GRADED not sharp (the bulk is not 0) and it
FADES with noise -- so the boundaries are soft and noise-dependent, not hard lines.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r data}
AID <- "Probit_statistics_Affine_Invariant_Distance_Likelihood_Information_Distortion"
NOISES <- c("0.1", "1", "10")
rd <- function(z) map_dfr(Sys.glob(sprintf("../data/433ed13/figure_3_nch_*_nsim_10000_macro_IR_noise_%s_battery_sim.csv", z)),
                          ~ read.csv(.x, skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)) %>% mutate(noise = z)
dd <- map_dfr(NOISES, rd) %>%
  filter(component_path == AID, statistic == "value", probit == "mean") %>%
  group_by(noise, Num_ch, interval_in_tau) %>% summarise(v = mean(value), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(is.finite(v)) %>%
  mutate(lx = log10(interval_in_tau), ly = log10(Num_ch),
         noisef = factor(paste("noise", noise), levels = paste("noise", NOISES)))

BR  <- c(0, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 1.0, 1.5)
PAL <- colorRampPalette(c("#f7fbff", "#fee0d2", "#fcae91", "#fb6a4a", "#de2d26", "#a50f15", "#67000d"))(length(BR) - 1)
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 7.6, fig.height = 3.4}
X_BRK <- log10(c(.01, .1, 1));           X_LAB <- c(".01", ".1", "1")
Y_BRK <- log10(c(10, 100, 1000, 10000)); Y_LAB <- c("10", "100", "1e3", "1e4")

g <- ggplot(dd, aes(lx, ly, z = v)) +
  geom_contour_filled(breaks = BR) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = PAL, drop = FALSE, name = "distortion distance\n(0 = Gaussian)") +
  geom_abline(slope = -1, intercept = 0, colour = "grey15", linewidth = .5, linetype = "dashed") +   # N_ch.Δ = 1
  geom_hline(yintercept = log10(30), colour = "grey15", linewidth = .5, linetype = "dotted") +        # N_ch = 30
  geom_point(size = .5, colour = "grey30", alpha = .55) +
  annotate("text", x = -1.75, y = 1.15, label = "N[ch]*Delta==1", parse = TRUE, size = 2.4, colour = "grey15") +
  facet_wrap(~ noisef) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = X_BRK, labels = X_LAB) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = Y_BRK, labels = Y_LAB) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-2, 0), ylim = c(1, 4)) +
  labs(x = expression(Delta / tau[min]), y = expression(N[ch]),
       title = "macro_IR residual distortion vs the domain boundaries") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 9, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), legend.position = "right",
        legend.title = element_text(size = 7.5), legend.text = element_text(size = 7),
        legend.key.size = unit(.32, "cm"),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 7), axis.title = element_text(size = 8),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 8), plot.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"))
g
ggsave("Figure_5_domains_validation.pdf", g, width = 7.6, height = 3.4)
```
